anchor.snippet: a generalized hypergeometric series is a power series in which the ratio of successive coefficients indexed by n is a rational function of n
The series, if convergent, defines a generalized hypergeometric function
A hypergeometric series is formally defined as a power series

annotations.label: Polynomial termination when a_j non-positive integer

anchor.snippet: If any a j is a non-positive integer (0, −1, −2, etc.) then the series only has a finite number of terms and is, in fact, a polynomial of degree
If any b k is a non-positive integer (excepting the previous case with b k < a j ) then the denominators become 0 and the series is undefined
If p < q + 1 then the ratio of coefficients tends to zero. This implies that the series converges for any finite value of z and thus defines an entire function of z
If p = q + 1 then the ratio of coefficients tends to one. This implies that the series converges for
If p > q + 1 then the ratio of coefficients grows without bound. This implies that, besides z = 0, the series diverges
It can be shown that the series converges absolutely at z = 1 if

the order of the parameters a j , or the order of the parameters b k can be changed without changing the value of the function
This cancelling is a special case of a reduction formula that may be applied whenever a parameter on the top row differs from one on the bottom row by a non-negative integer

annotations.label: Roots of x^n - x + t via hypergeometric functions

anchor.snippet: the roots of the polynomial x n − x +t can be expressed as a sum of at most
A generalization, the q-series analogues, called the basic hypergeometric series , were given by Eduard Heine in the late nineteenth century
Another generalization, the elliptic hypergeometric series , are those series where the ratio of terms is an elliptic function
Bilateral hypergeometric series are a generalization of hypergeometric functions where one sums over all integers, not just the positive ones

Fox–Wright functions are a generalization of generalized hypergeometric functions where the Pochhammer symbols in the series expression are generalised to gamma functions of linear expressions in the index n
